Off Calendar May-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Marissa Uchimura of MAUNE; RAICHLE; HARTLEY, FRENCH & MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for Petitioner Betty Dodge, the Guardian ad Litem for minors Ella Rogers and Kaytlyn Mae Rogers

RULING:
PETITIONER BETTY DODGE'S PETITION TO APPROVE COMPROMISE OF PENDING ACTION BY MINOR ELLA ROGERS AND THE PETITION TO APPROVE COMPROMISE OF PENDING ACTION BY MINOR KAYTLYN MAE ROGERS

Sign Language Interpreter Lixa Pustelnik was sworn to interpret for Betty Dodge, the guardian ad litem of minors Ella Rogers and Kaytlyn Mae Rogers. Both minors were also present in the courtroom. Petitioner Betty Dodge's petitions to approve compromise of pending action by minor Ella Rogers and Kaytlyn Mae Rogers were heard at the same time. Hearing held. The Court made the following rulings: Petitions to approve compromise of pending actions by minor Ella Rogers and minor Kaytlyn Mae Rogers are granted. Two orders approving the compromise and two orders to deposit money into the block account were signed in open court. The Court further ordered sealing the orders approving the compromise and the orders to deposit money. An order to seal was signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Bina Ghanaat of BURNHAM BROWN (510-835-6800), appearing for defendant / moving party Borgwarner Morse Tec Inc. as Successor-by-Merger to Borg-Warner Corporation
Laurel Halbany of MAUNE; RAICHLE; HARTLEY, FRENCH & MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Billy D. Rogers / Christina Rogers

RULING:
DEFENDANT BORGWARNER MORSE TEC, INC., AS SUCCESSOR-BY-MERGER TO BORG-WARNER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC Inc. as Successor-by-Merger to Borg-Warner Corporation's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action for false representation and intentional tort and claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant is liable pursuant to their causes of action for false representation and intentional tort. Plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Aug-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Laurel Halbany of MAUNE; RAICHLE; HARTLEY, FRENCH & MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Billy Rogers/Christine Rogers, et al.
Laura Przetak of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0425), Designated Defense Counsel
(See Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR A PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(b). Trial is December 12, 2013 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day of trial is December 18, 2013. Before any motions in limine are filed with the court the defendants are ordered to meet and confer on all motions in limine in order to avoid frivolous and duplicative hearings. If the parties do not meet and confer on the motions in limine the court will take appropriate actions. Last day for hearing summary motions is December 5, 2013. All parties stipulate to shorten to 35-day notice for summary motions. The additional two court days' notice requirement is waived such that E-Service is deemed equivalent of hand-service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than November 1, 2013. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 8, 2013.
